research_topic: Research E — Global, cultural, and resource-level comparison of health issue identification workflows
fact_summary: Diagnosis is a complex collaborative process involving patients, families, clinicians, systems, and tools.

fact_summary: OECD report estimates that up to 15% of diagnoses in OECD countries may be inaccurate, delayed, or wrong.

research_topic: Research E — Global, cultural, and resource-level comparison of health issue identification workflows
fact_summary: AHRQ reports an estimate of 795,000 Americans annually dying or becoming permanently disabled from disease misdiagnosis.

research_topic: Research E — Global, cultural, and resource-level comparison of health issue identification workflows
fact_summary: AHRQ PSNet ED review summary estimated roughly 1 in 18 ED patients receive an incorrect diagnosis.

research_topic: Research E — Global, cultural, and resource-level comparison of health issue identification workflows
fact_summary: HSSIB found GP systems did not reliably surface unresolved symptoms or repeated visits across different clinicians.

fact_summary: UK ombudsman identified repeated scan-reading and scan-follow-up failures, including abnormality not identified and results not properly followed up.

fact_summary: Israel has universal NHI coverage through four competing nonprofit health plans with strong health-plan EHR integration.

fact_summary: Bedouin communities in Israel face physical access, health-literacy, language/cultural, and out-of-system continuity barriers.

research_topic: Research E — Global, cultural, and resource-level comparison of health issue identification workflows
fact_summary: India has weak practical gatekeeping, a large public/private/informal ecosystem, slow EHR adoption, and no universal patient identifier.

research_topic: Research E — Global, cultural, and resource-level comparison of health issue identification workflows
fact_summary: A referral and free chest-x-ray intervention involving non-allopathic providers reduced TB diagnostic delay by 8.6 days.

fact_summary: China does not generally require referrals and ED information is not routinely sent to primary-care doctors.

fact_summary: Brazil Family Health Strategy teams cover defined households but specialty access remains a major bottleneck causing long queues and diagnostic delays.

fact_summary: Mexico has a segmented system with private providers delivering substantial outpatient care but poorly integrated into public networks.

fact_summary: WHO RAcE/iCCM trained CHWs to diagnose, treat, and refer childhood malaria, pneumonia, and diarrhea.

fact_summary: WHO Europe reports migrants/refugees face barriers including unclear entitlements, limited interpreters/cultural mediators, financial barriers, discrimination, and poor living conditions.
